task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sugar Write application interface should be visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Create a 'Learning Objectives' section with at least two objectives
task_completion.visual_evidence: The document contains the heading 'Learning Objectives' followed by at least two distinct listed items or sentences describing educational goals.

task_completion.description: Create a 'Daily Schedule' section with a 5-column table and at least 2 rows of activities
task_completion.visual_evidence: The document contains the heading 'Daily Schedule' followed by a visible table grid. The table must have 5 columns (labeled Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday) and at least 2 rows populated with activity text.

task_completion.description: Create an 'Assessment' section describing student progress measurement
task_completion.visual_evidence: The document contains the heading 'Assessment' followed by text describing how student progress will be measured or evaluated.

task_completion.description: Set the Sugar Journal entry title to 'Literacy Plan Week 3'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title bar at the top of the Sugar Write activity or the entry in the Sugar Journal interface displays exactly 'Literacy Plan Week 3'.

task_completion.description: Save the document as an ODT file to /home/ga/Documents/literacy_plan.odt
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save as', 'Export', or file manager dialog showing the file being saved to the path '/home/ga/Documents/literacy_plan.odt', or a terminal window verifying the file exists at that exact path.

integrity.description: The agent used the Sugar Write GUI to create the document rather than generating it via terminal scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent typing and formatting text within the Sugar Write graphical interface.

integrity.description: The agent used actual table formatting tools rather than simulating a table with spaces or tabs.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Daily Schedule' should appear as a structured grid with visible cell borders or standard word processor table formatting, not just text aligned with whitespace.

integrity.description: The agent generated actual lesson plan content rather than just copying the prompt instructions.
integrity.visual_evidence: The objectives, schedule activities, and assessment descriptions should contain plausible 2nd-grade literacy content (e.g., reading, phonics, spelling) rather than placeholder text or verbatim prompt text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file path is exactly '/home/ga/Documents/literacy_plan.odt'. The Sugar Journal entry must be titled exactly 'Literacy Plan Week 3'. The document must contain the exact headings: 'Learning Objectives', 'Daily Schedule', and 'Assessment'. The 'Daily Schedule' must be an actual table structure.
